inputs.value: A high-quality studio photograph of a simple and neat, with exactly 10 to 30 glass pieces, physical handcrafted stained glass suncatcher representing a cute friendly ghost wearing a pointed witch hat, isolated on a plain crisp solid white background. The ghost is depicted in a full body view with a smooth, rounded silhouette. Constructed from gorgeous pieces of textured translucent colored glass in milky white, soft lavender, and deep purple. All glass segments are bound by thick, continuous, smooth dark solder copper foil joints that completely partition the design into closed cells representing real pieces of glass, with absolutely no loose lines, no hanging lines, and no impossible narrow cuts. A structural solder seam must connect the ghost's eyes to the surrounding white glass to ensure stability. The witch hat is partitioned into four distinct segments: a curved top cone, a band, and two curved brim sections. The ghost's body is divided by vertical and curved solder lines that define its flowing draped fabric effect, with each line extending from the top edge of the hat's brim to the bottom undulating edge. Designed for realistic physical glass cutting and assembly, with extremely smooth easy-to-cut outlines, no narrow shapes, and no complex jigsaw lines, acting as a realistic glass fabrication template.
